assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with MIXED — both additive and reductive signals are present across the test file changes
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14 contains the summary table row: '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | Both additive and reductive signals present. Additive: new test function `test_recommend_purls_dedup` in modified file, new test file `tests/api/purl_simplify.rs` with 3 new functions. Reductive: removed test function `test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers`, relaxed assertion in `test_recommend_purls_basic` (changed from fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to versioned PURL without qualifiers). |'

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the removed test function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers as a reductive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table (line 14) states 'Reductive: removed test function `test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers`'. The structural scan table (line 36) shows '-1 (`test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers`)' in the Reductive column. The semantic assessment (line 61) elaborates: 'Removed function `test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers`: This function tested that PURLs with different qualifiers were returned as separate entries with qualifier details visible in the response.'

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings note the assertion change in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers, which the semantic assessment may identify as a relaxation contributing to the MIXED classification
assertion_results.evidence: report.md structural scan (line 38) identifies: 'Assertion specificity | 0 | -1 (in `test_recommend_purls_basic`: assertion changed from exact match on fully qualified PURL `"pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12?repository_url=https://repo1.maven.org&type=jar"` to shorter versioned PURL `"pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12"`)'. The semantic assessment (lines 63-64) calls this 'assertion weakening without count change' and notes 'the original assertion was strictly more specific -- it verified the exact qualifier content, not just its absence.' This is explicitly identified as contributing to the MIXED classification.

assertion_results.text: The new test file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs is recognized as additive (3 new test functions)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table (line 14): 'new test file `tests/api/purl_simplify.rs` with 3 new functions'. Detailed analysis (lines 44-51) lists the new file with '+3 (`test_simplified_purl_no_version`, `test_simplified_purl_mixed_types`, `test_simplified_purl_ordering_preserved`)' test functions added. Line 50 states 'All signals | Purely additive (new file)'.

assertion_results.text: The new test function test_recommend_purls_dedup in the modified file is recognized as an additive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table (line 14): 'Additive: new test function `test_recommend_purls_dedup` in modified file'. Structural scan (line 36) shows '+1 (`test_recommend_purls_dedup`)' in the Additive column for the modified file `tests/api/purl_recommend.rs`. Semantic assessment (line 67): 'New `test_recommend_purls_dedup`: Tests entirely new behavior (deduplication after qualifier removal) that did not exist before. This is genuine new coverage.'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report files: report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. These are all analysis/verification documents. The report discusses code changes that were part of the PR under review, but the skill itself produced only report files. No code files (e.g., .rs, .toml, .json source files) appear in the outputs directory, and no output file contains evidence of the skill modifying any source code.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md presents verdicts ('Overall: PASS') and detailed analysis but contains no merge commands, no gh pr merge invocations, and no indication of an automated merge action. The report structure (summary table with Check/Result/Details columns, detailed analysis sections) is informational, presenting findings for human review. The closing line states the findings are 'informational' regarding test classification.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification verdict appears in the verification report summary table and is accompanied by a detailed analysis section explaining the structural and semantic assessment
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14 contains 'Test Change Classification | MIXED' in the summary table. Lines 25-71 contain '### Test Change Classification -- Detailed Analysis' with two subsections: '#### Structural Scan' (lines 27-55) containing tables of additive/reductive signals for both the modified and new test files, and '#### Semantic Assessment' (lines 57-71) providing explanatory analysis of each signal's significance.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 31 states 'Comparing base-branch version against PR-branch version:'. The structural scan tables (lines 34-42) enumerate specific function additions/removals (test_recommend_purls_dedup added, test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers removed), assertion additions/removals with counts, and assertion specificity changes referencing exact PURL strings from test file content. The semantic assessment (lines 59-71) analyzes specific test file content (function behavior, assertion strings like 'pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12?repository_url=...'). While it mentions task alignment as context, the classification itself is derived from structural comparison of test file content between branches.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13 states: 'Test Quality | PASS | Eval Quality: N/A. All test functions have doc comments. No repetitive test patterns detected requiring parameterization.' The Review Feedback row (line 5) confirms: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No reviews or comments exist on the PR'. Since no reviews exist on the PR at all, no eval result reviews can exist, making Eval Quality N/A. The Test Quality verdict is PASS without being affected by Eval Quality.
